name: Cranberry-Orange Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Spray a large or two small cookie sheets with nonstick cooking spray.
3. In a large bowl, combine sugar and brown sugar; beat until well blended. Add margarine and orange peel; beat again. Stir in eggs; mix well.
4. Sift flour into a separate cup; add baking powder and stir into the batter. Fold in cranberries and almonds.
5. Shape dough into three equal rolls, about 7 inches long. Place rolls at least 3 inches apart on sprayed cookie sheets; flatten each to form rectangles that are 3/4 inch thick, 3 inches wide, and 7 inches long.
6. Bake for 18-25 minutes or until golden brown and firm in the center. Cool on wire racks for a few minutes.
7. Cut each rectangle into 1/2-inch slices; place cut sides up on the sheets.
8. Bake for an additional 6-8 minutes, turning once, until tops are slightly dry.
9. Turn out cookies and let cool completely on racks.
10. In a small bowl, whisk together powdered sugar, remaining orange peel, and milk until desired drizzling consistency is reached.
11. Drizzle glaze over cooled cookies; allow to set before serving.
This recipe yields about 4 dozen biscuits with approximately 80 calories per cookie (20 calories from fat, 2 grams of fat).
